label: Spurious double consonant
description: A single consonant wrongly written double (dopo -> doppo, sabato -> sabbato, camera -> cammera, capitale -> cappitale). The mirror of geminate_simplified: over-application of the doppie rather than under-application. Together they make doubling a two-way discrimination.

label: Digraph reduced to a plain letter
description: Spelling a gl / gn / sc digraph as a single letter or letter + i, losing its /ly/, /ny/ or /sh/ value: famiglia -> familia, figlio -> filio, bagno -> banio, prosciutto -> prociutto. Systematic L1 transfer where the reader's language has no such digraph.

label: qu / cu confusion
description: Writing qu for cu or cu for qu in the /kw/-family: scuola -> squola, cuore -> quore, and the qui (here) / cui (whom) swap. The /kw/ sound is spelt qu by default but cu in a closed lexical set.

label: cqu family mis-spelled
description: Reducing or mis-forming the small cqu cluster (acqua -> aqua / acua, acquisto -> aquisto, nacque -> naque) OR over-extending it to a plain-qu word (aquila -> acquila). cqu is a near-closed set: acqua and derivatives, plus a handful of passato-remoto forms (nacque, piacque, tacque).

label: Silent h added (hypercorrection)
description: Adding avere's silent h to a word that has none: anno (year) -> hanno, a (to) -> ha, o (or) -> ho. The insertion mirror of the existing silent_h_omitted; both directions are live because each avere form has a real homophone.
examples: un hanno fa (for un anno fa)

label: Elision apostrophe on the wrong gender
description: Putting the un' apostrophe on a masculine noun (un'amico, un'esame) or omitting it on a feminine one (un amica). The apostrophe marks feminine una-elision only; masculine un is a troncamento (truncation) that takes no apostrophe. The single sharpest article-spelling contrast.

label: Spurious apostrophe
description: An apostrophe written where standard orthography has none. The flagship is qual'è for qual è: qual is a troncamento (not an elision), so nothing is dropped and no apostrophe belongs. Also covers writing po' as an apostrophe'd form correctly but adding stray apostrophes to truncated words that don't take one.

label: Over-capitalisation (English transfer)
description: Capitalising mid-sentence what Italian lower-cases: weekdays (Lunedì), months (Gennaio), languages and nationalities as adjective or noun (Italiano, gli Italiani), and courtesy titles (Signor Rossi). Pure L1-transfer from English capitalisation habits.

label: Unstressed vowel confusion
description: Lexical substitution of unstressed vowels: i/e (difficile -> difficele, dicembre -> decembre, minuto -> menuto) and o/u (comprare -> cumprare, novembre -> nuvembre). Item-specific rather than rule-governed; distinct from the morphological io_plural_i_count and i_retention_error already in the registry.
